title: Clinical Safety & Shifts
concept: Deploying medical models requires strict clinical validation, FDA regulation, and defenses against domain shifts caused by different scanners.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What does the STAPLE algorithm do?

options.text: It calculates a weighted consensus label based on annotator reliability
It crops 3D scans into 2D slices automatically
It increases the learning rate of the optimizer

exercises.explain: Correct! STAPLE is a weighted consensus method for multi-annotator data.

cards.title: The hospital transfer failure
cards.body: You train a liver segmentation model on CT scans from Hospital A's scanner. It gets 95% accuracy. You deploy it at Hospital B, which uses a different scanner brand. Suddenly, the model's accuracy drops to 60%! The scans look slightly different in contrast, and the model fails. This is a domain shift, and in medicine, it can be dangerous.

cards.title: Domain Shift and FDA Regulation
cards.body: Domain Shift occurs when a model is tested on data from a different distribution (e.g. scanner brand, patient population) than its training data. Because of this, medical models must pass strict clinical trials and receive regulatory approval (like **FDA clearance**).

cards.title: Driving in a new country
cards.body: A model trained on one scanner is like a driver who only knows the streets of their small hometown. Putting the model in a new hospital is like forcing that driver to navigate a busy roundabout in a foreign country in the rain — they will likely make mistakes.

cards.title: Scanner variance
cards.body: Hospital A uses a GE MRI scanner with specific contrast settings. Hospital B uses a Siemens scanner. The gray levels of the organs differ slightly. The model, sensitive to absolute gray values, misidentifies tissue boundaries.

cards.title: Offline testing is not enough
cards.body: You might think a model is ready for clinical use if it has a high Dice score on your test set. That's a myth! Medical models must undergo **clinical trials** on fresh, prospective patients at multiple hospitals to prove they are safe under real-world shifts.

left.text:
Scanned on training hardware
Sharp, correct organ masks
High confidence

right.text:
Different scanner brand or contrast
Fuzzy, incomplete, or shifted masks
High error rate

exercises.prompt: What is domain shift in medical imaging?

options.text: A change in data distribution (like different scanners) that causes model performance to drop
A method for moving files between different server directories
The speed at which radiologists draw bounding boxes
A technique for converting 2D images to 3D volumes

exercises.explain: Correct! Domain shift is a major hurdle for medical AI generalization.

exercises.prompt: True or False: FDA clearance for a medical AI model guarantees it will perform perfectly on any scanner brand.
exercises.explain: False. Clearance means it is deemed safe and effective, but domain shifts can still degrade performance in new hospitals.

exercises.sentence: To prove a medical model's safety, it must undergo clinical validation at ___ hospitals.
exercises.explain: Testing at multiple hospitals is required to verify the model generalizes across scanner brands.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a common cause of domain shift in CT scan segmentation?

options.text: Using a different scanner manufacturer or scan protocol at a new hospital
Setting the batch size too low during evaluation
Using a hybrid loss function instead of cross-entropy

exercises.explain: Scanner differences modify the image characteristics, confusing the model.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a chest X-ray segmentation model. How can you protect your model from domain shifts before running it on scans from a new clinic?

options.text: Apply intensity normalization (like Z-score normalization) and evaluate the model on a small pilot dataset from the new clinic first.
Train the model on color JPEG images from the internet.
Disable the decoder branch and use only the raw encoder features.
Increase the learning rate during testing to adapt to the new images.

exercises.explain: Perfect! Normalizing image intensity distributions and running pilot validation tests are critical safety steps when deploying medical AI across different clinics. Next: how do we evaluate segmentations! Phase 9: Image Segmentation is complete. Next up: Advanced Vision Applications.
